purpose: Davao City source-target menu for the four earthquake-only answerability questions. This identifies the strongest Davao-first document names, public sources, manual custodians, search terms, and overclaim boundaries.
scope: Davao City earthquake, NSCP/seismic design evidence, OBO structural permit/review evidence, post-earthquake official tag/status, and post-damage or post-tag clearance/reoccupancy evidence.
global_rule: These sources and document names help answer whether evidence exists. They do not certify that a building is safe, unsafe, earthquake-proof, NSCP-compliant, noncompliant, permitted, or unpermitted.

primary_davao_custodian.office: Davao City Office of the City Building Official (OCBO)
known_functions: Complaints on violations against the National Building Code
primary_davao_custodian.manual_request_path: Use the Davao Citizen's Charter service 'Issuance of Certified True Copy of Documents' and request the exact target-specific record by building/subscope and date.
manual_request_requirements_from_charter: Photocopy of Building Permit and other related documents
Photocopy of Valid ID of the Owner with three signatures
If not the owner, processor valid ID with three signatures and original Special Power of Attorney
If lost, original Affidavit of Loss

secondary_custodians.use: Public-building inspection reports, unsafe/not-safe-for-occupancy findings, repair recommendations, and inspection reports after earthquakes.
Damage reporting intake and referral path for cracks, leaning/tilting structures, jammed doors, and other post-earthquake building damage signs.
Technical inspection support. Treat as official answer evidence only when OCBO, LGU, City Engineer, or DPWH publishes or adopts the result.
National rapid visual screening, placard, and National Building Code process context. Usually not a Davao target-specific custodian.

questions.davao_question: Is there target-specific evidence that this Davao building was designed or reviewed for NSCP earthquake/seismic requirements?
strongest_answerable_targets: Target-specific signed/sealed Seismic Analysis
Target-specific signed/sealed Structural Design Analysis or structural design computations
Target-specific Civil/Structural Permit Form with Civil/Structural Plans, only if issued, approved, released, or tied to OCBO review
Target-specific approved plans and documents stamped with Building Permit Number
Target-specific OCBO Evaluation Sheet showing structural review
Target-specific issued Building Permit that references approved structural/civil documents or structural review
Target-specific Civil/Structural Designs, Computations, Plans and Specifications
process_or_manual_leads_only: Davao Citizen's Charter checklist
Blank Building Permit or Civil/Structural forms
Boring and Plate Load Test Result/Report without structural/seismic design analysis
PRC ID or PTR of signing professionals without the signed/sealed target document
OBPS portal page without a visible issued/approved target record
Certificate of Annual Inspection of Buildings without original design/review evidence
davao_search_queries: site:davaocity.gov.ph "approved plans and documents" "Building Permit Number"
site:davaocity.gov.ph "Certified True Copy of Documents" "Office of the City Building Official"

questions.davao_question: Is there target-specific evidence that this Davao building went through official OCBO structural permit or plan-review processing?
strongest_answerable_targets: Target-specific issued Building Permit
Target-specific approved/released Civil/Structural Permit Form with Civil/Structural Plans
Target-specific approved plans/documents stamped with Building Permit Number
Target-specific Evaluation Sheet showing structural review
Target-specific Comprehensive Correction Sheet or Notice of Disapproval, with answer limited to review happened and not approval
Target-specific Certificate of Occupancy tied to approved permits/plans
Target-specific Approved Permits including Building Construction and Civil/Structural permits
Target-specific Approved plan of Building Permit
process_or_manual_leads_only: Blank permit application form
Davao Citizen's Charter process page
Order of Payment without issued permit, approval, or review disposition
Certificate of Annual Inspection of Buildings without original permit/review records
OBPS portal without visible target-specific issued/approved record
davao_search_queries: site:davaocity.gov.ph "Seismic Analysis" "Office of the City Building Official"
site:davaocity.gov.ph "Building Permit Number" "approved plans and documents"

questions.davao_question: After a specific earthquake, what is the official Davao tag or inspection status for this building or affected portion?
strongest_answerable_targets: City Government of Davao article, post, or list naming the building and tag/status
OCBO red tag notice or official red-tagged list
OCBO yellow tag notice or official yellow-tagged list
City Engineer's Office inspection result naming the building as unsafe for occupancy, not safe for occupancy, restricted, cordoned, or usable
Official OCBO/PICE rapid visual inspection result, only if OCBO/LGU publishes or adopts it
Official clearance/lifting update that states the new status after the earthquake
questions.green_tag_rule: Do not require the word green for Davao. National DPWH placard language supports green/yellow/red, but Davao official web sources found in this run mainly use red/yellow plus safe/not-safe-for-occupancy and clearance language. Accept green, inspected, safe for occupancy, cleared, or equivalent only if an official source says it for the exact target/date/scope.
process_or_manual_leads_only: Reputable news without retrievable official source
Operator says open or business as usual
PICE inspection mention without OCBO/LGU publication or adoption
No public tag result after a reported inspection
davao_search_queries: site:davaocity.gov.ph OCBO red yellow tag earthquake building Davao
site:davaocity.gov.ph "not safe for occupancy" earthquake Davao building
site:davaocity.gov.ph "cleared by the OCBO" earthquake building

questions.davao_question: If the Davao building was tagged, damaged, closed, or restricted, is there target-specific official clearance allowing use or re-occupancy?
strongest_answerable_targets: Official OCBO/LGU notice saying the building was cleared by OCBO after red/yellow tag, damage, or repair
Official notice saying re-occupancy, occupancy, entry, or use may resume for the exact building or affected area
Official green-tag update or tag lifting notice, if Davao uses or releases one for the target
Official final inspection, repair/retrofit acceptance, certificate of completion, or compliance acceptance that explicitly allows use or re-occupancy
Signed/sealed structural stability or structural soundness certificate after earthquake/repairs, with caveat if no OCBO/LGU clearance accompanies it
davao_terms_to_search: comply with OCBO conditions and safety requirements
process_or_manual_leads_only: Repair permit without completion or clearance
Approved repair methodology without final clearance
Comprehensive structural assessment report submitted to OCBO without acceptance or reoccupancy language
Notice to comply
Compliance report without authority acceptance
PICE-assisted inspection mention without OCBO/LGU adoption
Owner/operator reopening statement
Business hours, bookings, mall reopening, or tenant activity
davao_search_queries: site:davaocity.gov.ph OCBO red tag yellow tag cleared re-occupancy
site:davaocity.gov.ph "cleared by the OCBO" building earthquake

strongest_sources.name: Davao City Citizen's Charter 2026 Volume II
strongest_sources.use: Davao OCBO exact document names for building permit, occupancy permit, annual inspection, certified true copy, civil/structural plans, seismic analysis, evaluation sheet, Building Permit Number, and certificate processes.

strongest_sources.name: Davao City OCBO department page
strongest_sources.use: Primary Davao custodian and scope: building permit, occupancy permit, annual inspection, certification, and NBC complaints.

strongest_sources.name: Davao City issues red, yellow tags on earthquake-affected buildings
strongest_sources.use: Official Davao OCBO/PICE rapid visual inspection and red/yellow tag language.

strongest_sources.name: OCBO: Tagged buildings must comply with safety requirements
strongest_sources.use: Official Davao red/yellow tag meanings, comprehensive structural assessment, repair methodology, repair permit, and OCBO clearance language.

strongest_sources.name: OCBO urges immediate compliance with safety requirements for tagged buildings
strongest_sources.use: Official Davao building-specific red/yellow tag list and subscope examples.

strongest_sources.name: CEO identifies buildings unsafe for occupancy after October 4 quake
strongest_sources.use: City Engineer inspection language for unsafe/not safe for occupancy, repair recommendations, and structural design analysis context.

strongest_sources.name: Davao City LGU: Report signs of building damage
strongest_sources.use: Davao damage reporting path through CDRRMO, OCBO, and Davao City Reports.

strongest_sources.name: Davao Online Building Permit System
strongest_sources.use: Applicant/owner online path for building permits and occupancy permits; not a public proof source unless it exposes a target-specific issued/approved record.

strongest_sources.name: JMC 2018-01
strongest_sources.use: National building permit and certificate of occupancy process context.

strongest_sources.name: DPWH NBCDO
strongest_sources.use: National Building Code context and national process references.

strongest_sources.name: DPWH Department Order No. 027 s. 2023
strongest_sources.use: Rapid visual screening and placard context for green/yellow/red terminology.

overclaim_boundaries: A Davao checklist proves required documents, not that a specific building passed review.
A blank permit form is not evidence that a building was reviewed.
A Building Permit or Certificate of Occupancy must be target-specific and tied to the relevant building/subscope.
A red/yellow tag is date/scope-specific and must not be converted into a permanent safety claim.
A green tag, inspected placard, or clearance is date/scope-specific and must not be converted into an earthquake-proof claim.
A repair permit, repair methodology, or comprehensive assessment report is not clearance unless OCBO/LGU accepts it and allows use or re-occupancy.
PICE involvement is technical support unless OCBO/LGU/DPWH adopts or publishes the result.
Missing public records mean manual verification is needed, not that the building is unsafe, uncleared, unpermitted, or noncompliant.
